text:
If you’ve felt the ground shifting under “prompt engineering,” you’re not imagining it. Two powerful ideas now define how to get real business value from LLMs:

Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) make models smarter without retraining by evolving the context itself.

K.E.R.N.E.L. https://t.co/K9U7MTiu2T
thread:
If you’ve felt the ground shifting under “prompt engineering,” you’re not imagining it. Two powerful ideas now define how to get real business value from LLMs:

Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) make models smarter without retraining by evolving the context itself.

K.E.R.N.E.L. https://t.co/K9U7MTiu2T

1) RIP Fine-Tuning. Welcome to Self-Tuning.

Stanford’s ACE shows you don’t need to touch weights to improve performance:

The model writes → reflects → rewrites its own instructions.

Failures become strategies; successes become reusable rules.

Results reported: +10.6% on

2) K.E.R.N.E.L.: Six Patterns That Actually Matter

From 1,000+ real prompts, K.E.R.N.E.L. captures what consistently boosts quality and reliability:

K — Keep it Simple: One clear goal beats 500 words of fluff.

E — Easy to Verify: Define success criteria (“include 3 code

3) The ACE + K.E.R.N.E.L. Workflow (Copy/Paste)

Use this for agents, RAG apps, or internal copilots.

Step A — Baseline (K.E.R.N.E.L.)

Context: relevant facts, files, or schema.

Task: one job only (e.g., “draft customer-ready summary”).

Constraints: tone, policy, tools

4) A Concrete Template (Works for most use cases)

This single block gives you K.E.R.N.E.L. clarity and an ACE feedback loop that improves itself. https://t.co/MWFJ7UQg1J

5) Where to Use This (High-ROI Spots)

Support & Success: ticket summaries, root-cause digests, next-best actions.

RevOps & Sales: opportunity notes, call recaps, proposal redlines, pricing checks.

Finance Ops: invoice QA, expense audits with rule learning, close checklists.

6) Common Failure Modes (and Fixes)

Too many goals in one prompt → split tasks; chain steps.

Vague acceptance → add measurable criteria (lengths, counts, thresholds).

Playbooks bloat → archive stale rules; cap edits per run.

Drift across teams → version playbooks, require

7) Executive Takeaways

Stop defaulting to fine-tuning. Try ACE with K.E.R.N.E.L. first; it’s faster, cheaper, and easier to govern.

Treat prompts like software. Version, test, review, promote.

Invest in context ops. Your playbooks become the moat, portable across models and

TL;DR

ACE turns prompts into living systems that learn.

K.E.R.N.E.L. makes each prompt clear, verifiable, and shippable.

Together, they deliver production-grade reliability—without retraining models.

If you want the ready-to-use ACE + K.E.R.N.E.L. Notion kit (templates, eval

text:
Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) is here!

A new framework for self-improving LLMs by evolving contexts as dynamic playbooks, preventing collapse and outperforming baselines on agent and domain-specific tasks. https://t.co/kCTSAbhaLi
thread:
Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) is here!

A new framework for self-improving LLMs by evolving contexts as dynamic playbooks, preventing collapse and outperforming baselines on agent and domain-specific tasks. https://t.co/kCTSAbhaLi

ACE uses generation, reflection, and curation to adapt contexts without labeled supervision. It boosts performance by +10.6% on agents and +8.6% on finance, even matching production-level agents on AppWorld with smaller models!
Read more: https://t.co/8deJbzQCvS
